Displays better-than-expected wiggle in space as he's able to string together open-field moves to maximize runs. Above-average tester for a QB per sophomore numbers (4.89 40, 4.45 shuttle). State title winner with big production as a junior. Impressed at Elite 11 Finals in Summer 2020. Velocity consistency has improved in senior season. Generates a fastball in the short-to-intermediate passing game. Often victimized by drops but completion percentages as a junior and senior are a concern. Can improve footwork consistency. Has technical room to improve and should do so with a strong tool set for the QB position. Projects to the high-major level with multi-year starter potential and an NFL Draft ceiling.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2019: led Shadow Creek to a 16-0 record and the Texas 5A D-I title as a junior; named state championship game Offensive MVP.

name: Preston Stone
scouting_report: Owns the requisite size of a high-major QB prospect. Above-average height with a good build. Consistently accurate passer and increasingly so over high school career. Gets the ball out pretty quickly in the short passing game. Excels in short-to-intermediate situations. Consistent overhead release aids accuracy thanks to repeatability. Good pocket feel. Climbs the pocket to step into throws when required. Also shows promising knack for off-schedule playmaking ability. Functionally athletic enough to gain yards with legs but priority is to find receivers when breaking the pocket. Can hit receivers on the move with and across his body. Varies arm angles as needed. Willing to stand and deliver under the gun. Can drop it in the bucket in the vertical game. Winner who led team to consecutive state championships in Texas' largest private school division as a junior and senior. Staggering four-year production with 13,000-plus passing yards and 2,000-plus rushing yards. Consistency in velocity can improve. Takes chances that will backfire vs. college competition. Will face significant jump in competition going from Texas private school ranks to FBS. Seems like a particularly high-floor QB prospect with a lot of polish, but that could also limit long-term ceiling. One of the top 2021 QB's in a loaded class in Texas. Likely a reliable multi-year college starter with potential to reach the NFL Draft.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: As a senior, led Dallas Parish Episcopal to a second consecutive D-I state championship in Texas Association of Private and Parochial Schools.2019: Led Dallas Parish Episcopal to D-I state championship in TAPPS.2017: Second-team MaxPreps Freshman All-American.

name: Garrett Nussmeier
scouting_report: Adequate height with an average frame. Should be able to get north of 200 pounds. Provides outstanding production against strong competition at Texas 6A level. Accurate passer to all levels. Shows good touch on timing routes and other deep throws outside the numbers. Flashes playmaking ability to make impressive off-schedule throws under pressure. That translates not only in game situations, but select 7-on-7 environments as well. Pro-style but quick-footed and shows ability to make first rusher miss to extend play. Encouraging ability to throw on the run. Physical ceiling may be limited. Ball gets out fairly quickly, but tightening the delivery will help. Occasionally takes unnecessary chances. Talented QB with gunslinger mentality and great football pedigree. Among the top Texas QB's in 2021 class. Projects to high-major level with possibility for long-term NFL Draft potential.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2019: Earned District 6-6A Overall MVP honors as a junior; led Marcus to an 11-2 record and Texas 6A D-II third-round playoff appearance.Plays select 7-on-7 for Texas Flex based in the Dallas-Fort Worth Metroplex.

name: Sawyer Robertson
scouting_report: Tall, lean quarterback with ample space for adding good weight as development continues. Very productive in pass-heavy spread offense. Stays on top of the ball with mostly overhead delivery. Drives the ball down the seams, on throws to the middle of the field, and on outs. Really spins it in the short-to-intermediate range. Shows encouraging ball placement in tight windows, including back-shoulder throws. Nice touch and trajectory on deep balls, fades, and corner routes. Not a true dual-threat, but nimble enough to make the first wave miss to extend a play. Occasionally can surprise a defense with a designed run or scramble. Too flat-footed at times. Footwork needs to be more consistent. Can step into throws more consistently. Baseball mechanics occasionally manifest in a wind-up, hurting release quickness. Bona fide high-major prospect among the top QB's in a loaded 2021 TXHSFB class. Possesses long-term NFL Draft ceiling.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2019: Texas  District 2-5A-I first-team selection as a junior; led Lubbock Coronado to a 7-5 record and a Texas 5A D-I second-round playoff appearance.2018: Texas District 2-5A-I Offensive Newcomer of the Year honors as a sophomore.

name: Dematrius Davis Jr.
scouting_report: Undersized from a height standpoint but stout build with the look of a running back. Absolute gamer. Highly-decorated TXHSFB star for one of the nation's top programs. Generally accurate passer with numbers to support. Outstanding passing production with solid rushing numbers as a sophomore turned into good passing stats with terrific run numbers as a junior as role evolved. Two-time state title-winner going for his third in a row as a senior. Rare volume of big-game, high-leverage experience against elite high school competition. Intangibles are clear in those situations. Good off-schedule playmaker because of functional athleticism and awareness. Feels pressure pretty well in the pocket and is light on his feet and agile. Regularly makes first rusher miss. Creative playmaker who can improve consistency with short-to-intermediate velocity. Accurate on downfield throws outside the numbers, but consistency can get better to the short perimeter. Needs to stay on top of the ball better. Underwhelmed in elite camp settings next to other top QB's. Physical ceiling is limited. Type of player who creates many differing opinions on long-term potential. May not be a workout warrior, but is an unquestioned on-field leader and elite high school player with the trophy case to prove it. Flat-out football player who's not afraid of the moment. Projects to the high-major level with multi-year starter capability and perhaps could play beyond college.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2019: Led North Shore to a 15-1 record and second consecutive Texas 6A D-I state championship as a junior.2018: Named the MaxPreps National Sophomore of the Year and the District 21-6A Overall MVP; led North Shore to a 16-0 record and the Texas 6A D-I state title.

name: Clay Millen
scouting_report: Backed up his Pac-12 quarterback brother his first two years, so still has minimal varsity experience but put up big numbers in first year as a starter.  Boasts the arm strength, athleticism and play-making ability optimal for the position.  Makes the smart throws, plays within the context of his offense but can make plays when out of rhythm and off platform and isn’t afraid to take a few risks, with a little bit of a gunslinger mentality. Son of former NFL quarterback, has been around the game, and understands concepts and coverages. Loaded with physical upside. Projects as multi-year Power 5 starter and projects as late second day or early third day pick in the NFL draft.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundClay Millen is a 6-3, 188-pound Pro-Style Quarterback from Snoqualmie, WA.

name: Donaven McCulley
scouting_report: Tall, bigger-framed prospect. Very good athlete who can run and make plays with his feet. Throws well on the move and shows the ability to throw from every angle and does not have to be set to generate velocity. Above-average arm strength and can throw with touch. Has to keep working on getting set and getting rid of the ball quickly consistently. Although he was forced to scramble more based on protection. Could potentially play another position but has good upside as a quarterback. If the talent gets polished, he can start at a high-major level and have a chance to play beyond college.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundDonaven McCulley is a Quarterback from Indianapolis, IN.

name: Gavin Wimsatt
scouting_report: Owns good size and frame for the QB position with an athletic, somewhat high-cut build. Great length will allow for more mass in a college weight-training program. Multi-sport background in football, basketball and baseball. Played 58 games in basketball across freshman and sophomore years, averaging 11 points, six rebounds, and two blocks. Bouncy athlete with explosion on the hardwood. Functional athleticism shows on the gridiron as a play-extender outside the pocket and a runner, whether designed or scrambling. Not a burner but a long-striding speed-builder in the open field with good long speed relative to position. Generates a lot of torque and subsequent arm strength when throwing on the run, especially throwing across his body when rolling left. Displays good awareness in the pocket and has an instinctive play style that allows for big-play ability in off-schedule situations. Places the ball well on downfield throws outside the numbers. Arm strength does not look to be an issue. Size and athleticism allow for good over-the-middle velocity when required.  Strong but not elite production through sophomore and junior seasons. Accuracy and consistency can improve, as evidenced by 57 completion percentage over 2019-2020 seasons. Will need to cut down on turnovers (INT rate of one in every 29.36 throws over sophomore and junior seasons). Will take some chances with his arm at times, but that's also part of what makes him dangerous as a playmaker. Looks like one of the better 2022 quarterbacks nationally with a high developmental upside. Projects to high-major competition with the long-term potential to reach mid- to early-round NFL Draft consideration.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundThree-sport background in football, basketball, and baseball.2020: As a junior, led Owensboro to a 12-1 record and a Kentucky 5A state championship game appearance.2019: Breakout sophomore season saw Wimsatt lead Owensboro to a 12-2 record and a Kentucky 5A state semifinal berth.

name: Athan Kaliakmanis
scouting_report: Has good height. Still has a lot of room to fill in and needs to keep adding strength and bulk. Shows the arm talent to make all the necessary throws. Snappy, quick release with good ball speed and consistent zip on his short to intermediate passes. Athletic enough to escape pressure. Keeps plays alive and can make throws from odd angles without setting his feet. Calm under pressure and does not panic. Will stand in and make throws with pressure in his face. Good with his feet but not likely to be a consistent running threat in college. Still needs to keep working on reading defenses and making quick decisions while refining mechanics. Has physical tools and intangibles to be a Power 5 starter and develop into an NFL player.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAthan Kaliakmanis is a Quarterback from Antioch, IL.

name: Deacon Hill
scouting_report: Big, strong signal caller with a powerful down field arm. Might have the strongest arm in the ’21 class but is more then just a big arm, he’s a pure thrower who can change speeds and throws with timing and anticipation. Is accurate to all three levels of the field and boasts a quick, tight release and doesn’t need much of a wind up to get the ball down the field. Has worked hard to re-shape his body in the off-season and dropped about 20 pounds. Can still improve athletically and in his ability to extend plays and get outside the pocket but ins’t a bad athlete at all and does show good pocket presence and feel. The gene pool is strong in his family as well with three older sisters who played at the elite national level in water polo. He’s developing fast and shows the upside to be an upper tier Power 5 starter and future mid-high round NFL draft pick.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundDeacon Hill  is a Quarterback from Santa Barbara, CA.

name: Tyler Macon
school_info: East St. Louis (East St. Louis, IL)
scouting_report: Below average height but has a thicker, stocky build. Has the arm talent to make throws to different parts of the field. Can get it down the field and throws a nice deep ball. Has improved velocity and ability to hit deep outs and corners. Very tough physically and mentally. A leader that is cool under pressure. Has solid mobility. Does not quite classify as a dual threat but can get out of the pocket and make throws on the move. Has to get more consistent with his placement. Size may not be the prototype of a high-end pro prospect, but has too many intangibles to think he will not help a college team and potentially have a chance at playing on Sundays, especially considering how offenses are changing.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundTyler Macon is a 6-0, 180-pound Pro-Style Quarterback from East Saint Louis, IL.

name: Luke Altmyer
scouting_report: Relatively slight of frame quarterback though not to a concerning degree. Enough height and frame that has added good weight between junior and senior seasons. Controlled, accurate passer that plays with command in the intermediate game. Good touch with the ability to feather throws over second level defenders or make red zone pylon throws. Light active feet in the pocket. Highlight film shows eyes and feet married up through progressions. Has some outside the pocket ability and makes some slick plays against his body and off script. While he shows pace on throws in the middle of the field and in the short and intermediate areas of the field, he doesn’t show an elite arm on the big throws. Limited yards per attempt relative to the elite national quarterbacks. Despite really good combine athleticism, does not engage as dual-threat runner. We like as an ascending prospect that will be a Power Five starter who possesses some NFL upside. Further validation of physical tools could point to an even higher ceiling.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundVarsity Baseball

name: Carlos Del Rio-Wilson
scouting_report: Owns a solid build and average stature for the position. Should play in college around 215-220 pounds. Tests as a good athlete and backs that up on the field with rushing ability. Plays in one of the top classifications of Georgia High School football at a talented program. Has shown above average arm strength and ability to spin the ball in camp and 7-on-7 settings, perhaps more so than on video. Mechanically sound and has good feet in his drops and in moving throughout the pocket. Has some scheme versatility as he can operate in-structure within a pro-style, drop back offense while also running spread concepts. Can let his release get long at times and has a tendency to lock on his first read. Has shown some room for improvement with ball location in camp and 7-on-7 settings, missing low and behind at times. Projects as a Power Five starter.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundCarlos Del Rio is a Quarterback from Powder Springs, GA.

name: Sam Jackson V
scouting_report: Not a prospect who stands out physically on the hoof. Average height and needs to bulk up some. Does stand out on film as far as explosiveness and play-making ability. Fast, makes cuts at full speed and shows great ball skills for someone who has not played full-time receiver yet. Also has a strong arm at quarterback. The main question there is size as far as being a college signal caller. Too explosive and athletic though to not make an impact in college somewhere on offense. Even if it is as a receiver who plays quarterback part-time.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundExtensive experience at multiple positions, including QB and WR.

name: Kai Millner
scouting_report: Slight frame with narrow build, but room to add mass. Athletic quarterback with quick release and play-making ability. Compact and easily repeatable mechanics. Generates great torque without much wasted movement. Does well to slide up in pocket under pressure, but can also roll out to buy more time. Accurate to all levels, with knack for putting touch on throws over defenders. Impressive leader and high level competitiveness. Could improve overall arm strength and ball velocity. Upside as a multi-year Power Five starter and could garner NFL Draft attention.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundKai Millner is a 6-2, 188-pound Pro-Style Quarterback from Gilbert, AZ.

name: Sam Vidlak
scouting_report: Vidlak has plus pocket mobility, can make all variety of throws, in pocket and out of pocket.  Puts good touch on deep routes, zip on throws, particularly on intermediate routes. Can throw his receivers open, goes through his progressions and can extend plays to get through his reads. A little bit of a gunslinger with some risky throws.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundSam Vidlak is a Quarterback from Grants Pass, OR.

name: Shedeur Sanders
scouting_report: Highly productive and a winner with a pair of state championships to his name. Athletic frame, moves well and can extend plays. Does a nice job making that initial pass rusher miss, doesn’t panic and shows poise and calmness in the pocket. Has dual-threat ability and can pick up big chunks of yards with his legs but doesn’t take off and run as much as you would expect. Has enough arm strength to get the ball down the field and shows nice touch on his deep ball. Can tighten his release a bit but has some solid tools that project well to college. Upside is an impact upper tier Power 5 starter and potential NFL draft pick
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2019: As a junior, led Trinity Christian-Cedar Hill to a 13-1 record and a third consecutive Division II state title in the Texas Association of Private and Parochial Schools.2018: Led Trinity Christian-Cedar Hil to a 14-0 record and Division II state championship in the Texas Association of Private and Parochial Schools.

name: Christian Veilleux
scouting_report: Good frame and size. Pocket passer with mobility. Release is usually compact, especially on short throws. Ball comes out of hand freely. Accurate, particularly on short throws to edge and intermediate routes. Throws catchable ball with touch. Good footwork and base works in unison with arm. Gets through progressions. Plays composed. Drags ball a little at start of throwing motion. Improving arm strength will help on long throws. Adding strength will help. Sometimes relies more on touch than fully committing to throw. Has to learn to play under center. Multi-year starter at Top 20 program.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundChristian Veilleux is a Quarterback from Orleans, ON.

name: Heinrich Haarberg
scouting_report: Tall prospect who is lean now, but has the frame to be a big college quarterback. Excellent athlete. Has good straight line speed and can not only escape pressure, but is a legitimate running threat. Throws the ball with good touch and takes good care of the ball. Has a lower release and can add more snap to his release. Will need to adjust to the smaller windows in college. Has great upside, but is likely to need some developmental time. Physical tools and football savvy suggest he will be a Power 5 starter following those developmental years.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundHeinrich Haarberg is a 6-5, 185-pound Dual-Threat Quarterback from Kearney, NE.

name: Peter Costelli
scouting_report: True dual-threat quarterback and one of the fastest players in the region, regardless of position. Track standout who anchors his schools 4X100m relay team and has clocked a 10.8-100m. Once he breaks containment, he’s a threat to pick up huge chunks of yards and even go the distance. Very strong lower body and is tough to bring down in the pocket. Has improved his mechanics and tightened up his release. Arm strength is good and he throws a nice deep ball with touch and accuracy. Processing the game quicker is another area he has shown improvement and he can continue to get better in this area as well. Shows toughness and will stand in and take a shot in order to deliver the football. Projects as an impact upper tier Power 5 starter and potential NFL draft pick
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundPeter Costelli is a Quarterback from Rancho Santa Margarita, CA.

name: Justin Lamson
scouting_report: Versatile athlete, was an all-state safety early in career before taking over the reins at quarterback.  Strong build, fluid athlete and can run, with good pocket awareness and knowing when to tuck and run or stand tall in the pocket.  Can throw on the run.  Good job going through progressions and can make all the throws, short, intermediate and deep.  As he gets more starting reps should really flourish.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2019: Led Oak Ridge to an 11-3 record and a CIF Sac-Joaquin Section Division I runner-up finish.

name: Ari Patu
scouting_report: Patu has an athletic frame and the kind of body you might see in a small forward on a basketball team. He’s a mobile, dual-threat type quarterback with a smooth, easy delivery. He has plus arm strength and will make a big jump in this area as he fills out his frame. He’s very comfortable throwing on the run, whether it be rolling left or right and he can deliver the football from different arm angles. He’s not stiff or robotic like some young quarterbacks who have been coached up their whole life and why we think he makes a big jump once he hits college. He’s very fluid in how he throws the football and has the athleticism to escape and extend plays. He’s still raw in some area and will benefit from game experience after seeing limited reps as a junior. He’s a high character kid, very coachable and he has drawn rave reviews from those we have spoken to who are close to him. He's not a plug and play guy right now but with development, has an NFL upside to him for sure.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAri Patu is a Quarterback from Folsom, CA.

name: Jake Rubley
school_info: West Des Moines Valley (West Des Moines, IA)
scouting_report: Projectable build and frame that should take muscle. Has a big arm with smooth mechanics. Balanced thrower who is comfortable in the pocket. Stands tall to find targets downfield. Sets his feet well and plants a strong base to get everything behind his passes. Easy delivery, and ball comes out quickly. Accurately leads receivers. Athletic enough to gain yards when rolling out. Must refine drop-back footwork from under center. Potential all-conference contributor and multi-year starter at Power Five level. Upside as NFL Draft Day 3 potential.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundMoved to West Des Moines (Iowa) Valley for senior year. Previously played at Highlands Ranch (Colo.).

name: Charles Wright
scouting_report: Adequate size for the QB position, especially in today's game. Good production against Texas 6A competition. Flashes impressive accuracy and touch on deep throws outside the numbers, as well as red-zone throws to the end zone. Shows a knack for making off-schedule plays. Varies arm angle pretty well when needed. Not a home run hitter as a runner, but athletic enough to extend plays and hit the occasional designed run. Fairly smooth, consistent release. Can tighten delivery and quicken release a bit. Often throws with upper body alone and can more consistently uses his legs to help velocity and throw power. Projects to the Power Five level in a very deep 2021 QB class in Texas.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2019: Earned first-team all-district Texas 25-6A as a junior.2017: Played in four games on varsity as a freshman.

name: William Haskell
scouting_report: Lanky, wiry frame with high-upside build and plenty of room to add mass. Ultra athletic quarterback with mobility and knack for extending plays. Shows touch on downfield throws and can hit targets deep. Does well to step up in the pocket when facing pressure. Good zip on his passes, even when throwing off the wrong foot, but could add more velocity. Variety of arm angles and good anticipation. Exciting in the open field with ability to gain large chunks of yards. Could refine throwing motion and be more consistent on intermediate throws. Potential multi-year starter at Power Five level later in college career with chance to garner NFL Draft attention.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundWilliam Haskell is a Quarterback from Glendale, AZ.

name: Riley Leonard
scouting_report: Has a tall, slender build with room to add weight once he gets in a strength and conditioning program and focuses solely on football. Doubles as a top high school basketball player in the state of Alabama. Demonstrates touch and feel as a passer. Shows a good pocket instincts with the ability to slide up and avoid pressure while keeping his eyes downfield. Transfers his basketball athleticism over to the football field and uses his long stride to pick up yards as a runner. Needs to continue improving his arm strength and release time. Will have to add velocity to his throws to be productive with the smaller passing windows at the next level. Has the upside to develop into a starter at the Power Five level.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundRiley Leonard is a Quarterback from Fairhope, AL.

name: Xavier Ward
scouting_report: At 6-2, 200 pounds, he has a live, athletic body and can stress a defense with his arm or his legs. In terms of arm strength and his ability to spin the football, no one in the state is better. He’s a good athlete with the ability to extend plays and just needs more game experience and polish to really take his game to another level. He has huge hands and doesn’t look like he’s done growing yet and with continued development, he definitely has a chance to be among the top quarterbacks in the region.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundXavier Ward arrived at WSU as an early enrollee in January 2021

name: Will Crowder
scouting_report: Stands just over 6-foot-2 with a solid build. A confident thrower that isn’t afraid to push the ball down the field. Isn’t scared of tight windows, either. Shows plenty of touch, especially on deep passes. Owns a smooth, compact release. Comfortable in the pocket. Always trying to find a level base. Capable of picking up first downs with his legs and will break a tackle or two. Started games at the varsity level as a freshman after his older brother went down with an injury. Ended up leading his school to four consecutive playoff appearances. Can make all different types of throws, but success at the next level will likely be determined by accuracy and ability to quickly process defenses. Has the tools to start – and win – games on Saturdays.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso played baseball.2020: A member of AL.com's A-List, which ranks the Yellow Hammer state's top 15 recruits.2019: Went 8-4 as a starter leading his team to second round of 6A playoffs. Competed 185-of-303 passing attempts for 2,295 and 11 touchdowns to go against five interceptions. Also ran for 443 yards and five scores.

name: Aaron McLaughlin
scouting_report: Big, strong quarterback with advanced, mature frame. One of the best athletic profiles at the quarterback position in the 2021 class. Powerful thrower. Can make the big throws and deliver to the sideline. Sturdy in the pocket and can stand and deliver through a hit. Shows some impressive throws against his body on the run. Effective extending plays outside the structure of the offense. Big play ability as a runner. Tight thrower. Big frame creates a busy delivery that can be inefficient. Issues with accuracy and touch on short and intermediate routes. Nothing gaudy in terms of productivity, accuracy and efficiency. Has a lot of wow plays in his repertoire but needs to improve consistency. Could also be very talented tight end. Likely Power Five starter at quarterback with the athletic upside to play beyond college.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAaron Mclaughlin is a Quarterback from Cumming, GA.

name: Cameron Friel
scouting_report: Big frame and physical upside. Smart, instinctive quarterback with easy delivery. Impressive arm strength and accurate to all levels of the field. Shows patience in the pocket and steps into throws. Displays quiet mechanics, a smooth motion and quick release, although tends to drop his elbow at times. Flashes a great feel for pressure and can evade the rush with decent agility. Does well to time his throws over the middle while facing pressure. Has a nice feel in the red zone to fit passes between defenders. Could continue to improve on leading his receivers and going through progressions better. Potential multi-year starter at the Group of Five level with chance to garner some looks from NFL Draft scouts.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundCameron Friel is a Quarterback from Kailua, HI.

name: Luke McAllister
scouting_report: Big frame with ideal length and physical tools. Room to add mass, but checks off all boxes from stature standpoint. Strong armed quarterback and seamless thrower. Comfortable in the pocket and steps into his throws. Keeps eyes down field to find open targets and does well to hit receivers in stride. Shows knack for putting touch on the ball. Can fit throws into tight windows. Looks far more at ease rolling to his right and could improve footwork, ability to escape pressure and throw on the run. Room to refine mechanics, particularly with quicker release. Potential multi-year starter at Division I level with chance to garner looks from NFL Draft scouts.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundLuke McAllister is a 6-5, 200-pound Pro-Style Quarterback from Monument, CO.

name: Ben Easters
scouting_report: Good size all around. Tall with good weight. Should leave high school looking similar to quarterbacks on a Power 5 college roster. Above average arm strength. Can make throws to different parts of the field and has good touch on deep passes. Leads receivers away from defenders and can throw his receiver open. Makes quick decisions and gets the ball out of his hand quickly. Solid athlete who can run with the ball some and make throws on the move. Must improve completion percentage and limit turnovers. Has experience in a college-system. That, coupled with physical stature, should help him compete early and be a starter in time.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: Led Brownsburg to a 10-2 record and an Indiana 6A sectional playoff appearance.

name: Drew Allar
scouting_report: Owns excellent size with prototypical build of a big-bodied pro-style quarterback. Nearly 6-foot-5 and 230-plus pounds and wears it well. Arm strength reflects those physical tools. Ball gets out fast despite long-levered build. Displays big-armed vertical juice with terrific velocity in short-to-intermediate situations. Knows how and when to vary zip and does so from myriad arm angles with impressive playmaking creativity. Shows ability to suddenly and smoothly dial back power, if needed, at tail end of delivery. Not a true dual-threat, but capable of scrambling when the play breaks down. Throw power transfers to off-schedule, off-platform opportunities. Strong enough to make timely, accurate cross-body throws on the move. Functional athleticism derives in part from a foundation at other positions earlier in career. Shows anticipatory feel for the position. Strong production as a junior improved to outstanding during senior season. Only seven interceptions in 514 throws (one in 73.4 attempts) in 2021. May need time to adjust to speed of the game and processing rate at the next level. Most naturally talented thrower in the 2022 quarterback class. Not tapped out by any means and possesses immense upside. Should become an impact high-major starter with NFL Draft first-round potential.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2021: Led Medina to a 13-1 record and an Ohio Division I Region I runner-up finish.2020: Led Medina to an Ohio Division I Region I runner-up finish.

name: Cade Klubnik
scouting_report: Good height with a lean, athletic, somewhat narrow build. Mechanics have noticeably improved with reps and experience. A natural at the position. Competitive but calm and poised. One of the most accurate passers in the class whose ball placement and touch stand out. Good pocket awareness with functional athleticism to extend plays when necessary. Good athlete when he gets loose in the open field. Increasingly impressive improvisational ability as an off-schedule playmaker. Varies velocities and touch deftly. Can make all the throws, whether over-the-middle, intermediate bullets, or field-stretching deep balls. Outstanding production vs. Texas Class 6A competition for state championship-caliber team. Impresses in live game action and elite camp settings.  Competed in track and field (400 meters, triple jump, long jump) earlier in high school. Occasionally will take unnecessary chances, but owns an exceptional INT rate over a full two seasons starting. Undoubtedly one of the top 2022 quarterbacks in the country. Projects as a multi-year impact starter at the high-major level with NFL Draft high-round potential.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso has competed in track and field.Ran 51.52 in the 400 as a sophomore, while long jumping 19-10 and triple jumping 41-8.2020: Led Austin Westlake -- ranked No. 3 in the country by MaxPreps -- to a 14-0 record and the Texas 6A D-I state title as a junior; Texas District 26-6A Offensive MVP; MaxPreps first-team Junior All-American; Was named the TSWA Class 6A Offensive Player of the Year2019: Served as a reserve on Austin Westlake's 15-1 Texas 6A D-II state championship team as a sophomore.

name: Conner Weigman
scouting_report: Adept pocket passer that displays rhythmic timing and great touch on passes outside the numbers. Weigman has the intellectual capacity to handle checks at the line of scrimmage and in-play processing skills in a pro-style offense. Weigman does not have first round physical traits but has plenty of arm to make requisite throws. Weigman projects as a late round NFL draft pick with the upside to be a day two pick if he stays healthy, as he's been injured in 2023 and 2024.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundMulti-sport athlete who is also one of the top baseball prospects in Texas2021: Texas District 16-6A Offensive MVP as a senior; Named the Houston Touchdown Club Offensive Player of the Year; Named the National High School Quarterback of the Year by the National Quarterback Club2020: Was named the District 16-6A Offensive Player of the Year as a junior2019: Texas District 14-6A Offensive Newcomer of the Year as a sophomore.

name: Ty Simpson
scouting_report: Possesses adequate size for his position at 6-foot-1 plus and a shade under 200-pounds. Has growth potential to add additional weight to his frame at the next level. Displays good athletic ability in the pocket and above average mobility as a runner. Predominant pocket passer, comes from a spread run-pass option scheme in high school. Demonstrates good foot quickness and body quickness in the pocket, showing a natural ability to extend plays with his feet in or outside the pocket. Plays with good feel and instincts as a passer, keeping his eyes down field while withstanding pressure. Appears to have a clean and abbreviated throwing motion, demonstrating the ability to get the ball out quickly and from a variety of angles. Displays good arm strength as a passer, showing the ability to make every throw on the field. Flashes accuracy at every level possessing an ability to throw his receivers open and play with anticipation. Has a poise to his passing game in the pocket but also excels with his ability to improvise and extend plays with his feet. More of a passer, but you have to respect his feet as a runner. Rushed for over 1200 yards and 15 TD’s on the ground in his senior season. Balanced in both his abilities as a passer and runner, Simpson poses a multi-dimensional threat for opposing defenses at the next level, showing the ability to play within or outside the structure of an offensive attack. Projects to an all-conference, potential all-american prospect at the next level. Needs one season to sit and develop as he acclimates to the speed of the game at the collegiate level, but possesses the ability to be a multi-year high level performer for a Power Five program.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: Led Westview to a second-round playoff appearance in Tennessee Division I Class 3A as a junior.

name: Devin Brown
scouting_report: Long, wiry frame with room to add muscle mass. Carries 190 lbs. well and continues to fill out. Dynamic quarterback with basketball background. Shows very good arm strength and zip on his passes. Flashes ability to make off-platform throws without losing velocity. Distributes to all levels of the field and looks extremely comfortable rolling out to space. Displays good timing and anticipation over the middle. Mechanically sound and quick to release the football. Athletic and tough enough to plant his foot and gain yards with his legs. Could continue to improve decision-making and tendency to chase the big play. Needs to improve his touch and loft in tighter windows. Potential multi-year starter at elite Power Five level and second-day NFL Draft projection.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundPlayed at Queen Creek (Ariz.) before transferring to Draper (Utah) Corner Canyon for his senior season.2021: As a senior, led Corner Canyon to a 12-2 record and a Utah 6A state runner-up finish.2020: Led Queen Creek to a 6-3 record as a junior and an Arizona 6A first-round playoff appearance.2019: Varsity as a sophomore and helped Queen Creek to a 9-4 record and the Arizona 6A state semifinals.

name: Walker Howard
scouting_report: Adequate frame with lean build that should add some mass in college. Established a following as an elite camp performer early in high school career before taking over varsity starting duties. Has posted encouraging testing numbers early in career (4.76 40, 4.40 shuttle). Consistent, accurate passer to all levels. Steps into throws and gets great zip on balls to the short-to-intermediate range. Posted stellar production in breakout junior campaign. Threw a lot of reps while also valuing the ball, as evidenced by only one INT per 63 pass attempts (five in 316 throws). Not a true dual-threat but functionally mobile with ability to extend plays outside the pocket. Remains accurate on the move. Squares shoulders and fires even when on the run. Plenty of arm strength but still probably some juice to squeeze out at the top end, which should come with add physical mass/strength. Typically stays on top of the ball but will have one sail a bit every once in a while. Possesses a good QB pedigree. Seems quite polished relative to age and could be a particularly high-floor QB at the next level. Projects to high-major competition with long-term NFL Draft potential.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: Helped St. Thomas More to an 10-0 record and Louisiana Division I state championship in his first year as the starting quarterback.2019: Helped St. Thomas More to an 11-2 record and Louisiana Division I state championship as a sophomore.

name: Gunner Stockton
scouting_report: Thick built player with average height for the position.  He is a better athlete than on would think, He is able to make plays in the pocket when the rush breaks down by using his feel and presence for where the rush is coming from and how he needs to buy time. Does a good job of keeping his eyes downfield to make a play when he is flushed and on the move.  Shows good arm strength with the ability to make throws with touch when need be allowing for his receivers to make plays on the move to get extra yardage.  He has a quick release and is able to process if his 1st read isn't there to get through his progression. By throwing and running for a combined 4,709 yards passing and rushing along with 71 touchdowns as a junior you can see the growth in his game over that last couple of years.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso plays baseball.2020: MaxPreps Junior All-American second-team selection; led Rabun County to a 12-2 record and a Georgia AA state semifinal appearance.2019: MaxPreps All-American first-team selection as a sophomore; led Rabun County to a 12-1 record and a Georgia third-round playoff appearance.2018: MaxPreps Freshman All-American selection; led Rabun County to an 11-2 record and a Georgia AA third-round playoff appearance.

name: Maalik Murphy
scouting_report: Physically gifted signal caller with a lot of tools. Still needs game reps and experience to take his game to another level. Has a clean, easy delivery and one of the strongest arms in the class. Has tightened his release over the last year, less of a windup and gets it out quick. Shows good feet in the pocket but can continue to improve extending plays and getting out of the pocket to make a throw or running for plus yards. Has a big frame but has maintained his weight and isn't sloppy. Shows good touch on the deep ball and looks good when throwing on time and in rhythm. A natural leader who teammates will love in the locker room. Mature and works hard off the field. Very intriguing at the next level and has a ton of upside and if he maximizes his potential, has a chance to be special.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundMaalik Murphy is a Quarterback from Gardena, CA.

name: Brady Allen
scouting_report: A big high school quarterback who will have prototypical size as a college pocket passer. Varsity starter since early in his career and has a lot of game experience. Has the arm to make all the throws and has shown he can throw through wind and tough conditions without losing much velocity. Throws with good touch down the field and can drop the ball in over the top of coverage. Can also throw on the move without losing velocity. Elevated team throughout the course of his career and played well against good competition. Can throw on the run but is not as much of a running threat and has to show he can escape the rush at the Power Five level. Heavy shotgun in high school and may have to adjust to being under center more depending on college system. But a big prototype with a great arm that makes him a high end prospect and potential Sunday guy.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundBrady Allen is a 6-5, 196-pound Pro-Style Quarterback from Fort Branch, IN.

name: Tayven Jackson
scouting_report: Athletic passer with good physical traits. Has height and the body to fill in. Multi-sport guy who does athletic tasks fluidly and easily on the field. Mohile and elusive in the pocket. Can take off and make plays with his feet. Gets rid of the ball quickly, does not try to hold it too long or sandlot play despite his athleticism. Did not throw a high volume of balls in high school compared to other top quarterback recruits and must continue to polish pure passing skills and mechanics. May need a year or two in college, but has a high ceiling.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundTayven Jackson is a Quarterback from Greenwood, IN.

name: AJ Duffy
scouting_report: A gamer of sorts that has flashed in a variety of different settings. Has a knack for making something out of nothing. Creative when the pocket breaks down and does a nice job of keeping his eyes down the field while on the move. Equipped with a live arm that has proven to be rather accurate when he’s got his A game going. Not afraid to take a deep shot or challenge a tight coverage window. Started prep career off in California where he lost his entire junior year due to global pandemic. Ended up transferring into the national powerhouse that is IMG Academy for his senior season where he directed a run-heavy offense. Still managed to complete just under 64 percent of his passes with 13 touchdowns to go against four interceptions. Must cut down on mistakes and limit errant throws when he gets to college, but has a chance to win games at the Power 5 level one day given his skillset as he’s more than capable of making high-level throws. Likely to find the most success in a balanced single-back spread attack that has plenty of RPOs on the call sheet.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundSelected for Under Armour All-America Game and Elite 11 Finals…transferred to IMG Academy for senior season after junior year wiped out due to pandemic…as sophomore at Rancho Verde in California, led Mustangs to 11-1 overall record and quarterfinal round of CIF Division 2 Southern Section Championship…was 185-of-261 passing for 2,568 yards and 26 touchdowns while also adding 501 yards and four touchdowns rushing…helped lead Rancho Verde to 13 wins and CIF Division 2 Southern Section championship game during freshman season when he was 163-of-234 passing for 2,984 yards and 40 touchdowns with 316 yards and four touchdowns rushing.Finished high school career at IMG Academy in Bradenton, Fla. Originally from Inland Empire, Calif.

name: Holden Geriner
scouting_report: Intriguing quarterback prospect given quick release, sound decision making process and ability to push the ball down the field. Capped off prep career by wining a state title as a senior in Georgia’s AAAA classification. Stands tall in the pocket and does a nice job of feeling out pressure. Has some experience with five and seven-step drops, but seems to be at his best when he’s making one or two reads and then firing to a spot or target. Isn’t afraid to challenge tight windows. More of a drive ball thrower, but does show some touch on his deeper passes. Shouldn’t be considered a real dual-threat talent, but can extend plays once the pocket breaks down and pick up yardage with his legs in certain situations. Completed just over 67 percent of his passes his final two years of high school tossing 61 touchdowns to go against just six interceptions. Likely to excel in more of a balanced spread attack that wants its quarterback to distribute the football, and quickly. Will likely need some time to adjust to the speed of the game on Saturdays, but has the skillset to eventually emerge as someone that can win at the Power 5 level. Could eventually suit up on Sundays if he keeps progressing.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2021: Atlanta Journal-Constitution AAAA Offensive Player of the Year. Led Benedictine to a 13-2 record and a state title as he completed just under 68 percent of his passes for 3,377 yards with 36 touchdowns to go against just three interceptions. Threw for a season-high 448 yards against Ware County. Tossed four touchdowns against Carver.

name: Sam Horn
scouting_report: Excellent height for the position with a moldable frame. Baseball background. A true gunslinger equipped with a live arm that’s always looking to make a play. Simple delivery motion, releasing over-the-top. Puts plenty of pace on the ball, but also understands how to use touch. Can make a variety of different throws. Mobile athlete with the foot quickness to escape the rush. Relatively accurate on the move. Will make people miss in the open field. Needs to take better care of the football and cut down on the interceptions. Must also improve timing and get better at working through his progressions. Highly productive at the prep level playing in a pass-happy system. Helped carry his team to the state finals as a junior playing in one of the Peach State’s highest classifications. Has the looks of someone that will develop into a starter at a Power 5 program. Tools and athletic profile suggests that he has a chance to play on Sundays.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso plays baseball. Drawing interest from SEC schools. Older brother, Monty, is a pitcher at Kennesaw State.2020: Led Collins Hill to a 12-3 record and the AAAAAAA state final. Passed for over 3,900 yards and 41 touchdowns in a wide-open, pass-happy offense. Doubled as his team's punter.

name: Jacurri Brown
scouting_report: Impressive looking athlete with the desired height to play the position. Owns a leaner build now, but could eventually get up to 220 pounds or more. One of the more unique quarterback prospects in the class of 2022 given ability to run the football. Has already eclipsed the 100-yard mark rushing in 14 different varsity games. Still developing as a passer, but started to show some improvement the spring after junior season. Has no issues getting the ball to all different levels of the field. Likes to use trajectory to his advantage, especially on his deep shots. More of a drive-ball thrower, but capable of making off-plane throws on the run. Streaky player that’s not afraid to challenge tight windows, but struggles at times with accuracy and tends to make some of the easiest throws the most difficult. Tested exceptionally well before his senior season clocking a 4.6 in the 40-yard dash and a 4.4 in the short shuttle. Long stride allows him to pull away from defenses. Not afraid, however, to lower his shoulder and power through defenders, if needed. Considered a leader by those in his huddle and has won plenty of big games on Friday nights playing in one of the Peach State’s highest classifications. Will need to find some touch and keep improving his mechanics, but raw tools are valuable at the quarterback position, and he has a ton of them. Should be viewed as a high-ceiling, low-floor prospect that can make a difference at the Power 5 level with patience and the right coaching. Likely to thrive in an offensive system that embraces dual-threat quarterbacks and leans on his strengths.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: Georgia AAAAAAA all-state first-team selection. Helped carry Lowndes to a 10-2 record and the state semifinals. Named Winnersville Classic MVP after totaling 276 yards of offense and four touchdowns.2019: Georgia Sports Writers Association AAAAAAA all-state first-team selection as an athlete. Led the Vikings to a state championship game appearance compiling a 14-1 record as a sophomore.2018: Started games on varsity as a freshman.

name: Justyn Martin
scouting_report: Long 6-4 quarterback with a ton of long term upside if he reaches his potential. A gifted thrower with a big arm and easy velocity. The ball jumps out of his hand and he can make every throw on the field with ease. He needs to continue to improve in how he quickly he processes the game and in his decision making but that should  come with more game reps. Still a relatively late bloomer and his development has been hurt a bit by lack of game experience due to Covid shortened junior season. He has worked hard to tighten his release over the last year and his motion is more compact and quicker. He’s a good athlete, a former basketball player and is able to extend plays and take off and run for big yards. Throws a tight ball and shows good touch on the deep ball as well. When he’s decisive in his reads, he’s very good and as the game continues to slow down for him, he has a chance to be a special player at the next level.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundLimited track experience as a sophomore in Spring 2020.Spring 2021: Played an abbreviated junior season. Led Inglewood to a 4-0 record as the Sentinels outscored opponents 206-62.

name: Zach Pyron
scouting_report: Possesses a thick frame at 6-foot-2 and 215-pounds. Displays above average athleticism and avoid-ability in the pocket. Flashes adequate mobility as a runner. Shows the ability to get what’s there. Demonstrates a clean shoulder high motion. Appears to have adequate to above average arm strength. Shows the ability to make a variety of throws; displaying the ability to throw with touch down the field. Excels with a moving pocket. Can get the ball out from a variety of arm angles. Will need to improve accuracy and ball placement as he continues to develop. Has some “gamer” qualities to him. Value his instincts within the pocket and ability to make plays on the run.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: Led Pinson Valley to the Alabama 6A state title as a junior.2019: Starting QB as a sophomore, leading Fyffe to a second consecutive Alabama 2A state championship.

name: Nate Johnson
scouting_report: Speed, speed and more speed.  With an arm to boot.  Johnson is one of the fastest football players in the state,  not just quarterbacks, players, period, running a 10.56 100m this spring.  But lest you think he's just a running quarterback, he's more a quarterback that can run- Johnson still wants to make plays with his arm. He's got a unique skill set, with arm strength and a quicker, consistent release and a smooth motion.  He is boiling with upside and has all the natural physical tools to be a great quarterback.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundMember of 247Sports’ 2022 Freaks List.

name: AJ Swann
scouting_report: A touch passer with a quicker release and above average pocket presence. Has shown the ability to not only push the ball down the field, but also challenge defenders and tight windows at various points throughout his prep career. Measured 6-foot-2 the summer before senior season. Owns a thicker build as he’s hovering 200 pounds and uses that weight to advantage as he isn’t afraid to take a hit. Also has a knack for moving the chains with his feet the unconventional way when a play breaks down. Clean more times than not with his drops and likes to step into his throws in hopes of generating more velocity. Completed just over 61 percent of his passes as a senior while playing in more of a pro-style spread offense. Must cutdown on mistakes at the next level after tossing over 20 interceptions the past three years, but has the skillset to win games at the Power 5 level. Could find success in a number of different offensive systems, but likely to be the most comfortable in the gun directing a balanced attack.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso plays baseball.2021: Helped Cherokee make opening round of Peach State's AAAAAAA playoffs. Completed just over 61 percent of his passes for 2,043 yards with 22 touchdowns to go against 7 interceptions. Also ran for 83 yards and two more scores in 11 games.2020: Region 5-AAAAAAA Co-Offensive Player of The Year. Guided the Warriors to an 8-3 record and a first-round playoff appearance; Competed at the Georgia Elite Classic.

name: Tanner Bailey
scouting_report: Smaller framed quarterback at just over 6-foot tall, and about 190 pounds. Natural pocket passer with some functional mobility but not a downfield running threat. Immense intelligence passing the football and understanding spacing and timing. Has the right trajectory on the football. Anticipatory and above average accuracy. Very adept throwing the short to intermediate passing game and has nice touch on the outside planes. Easy stroke and release. Doesn't have a cannon but makes up for it with accuracy and ball placement. Makes a high percentage of advanced level seam throws between defenders. Tough player that took his team to a championship as a junior and played banged up.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2020: Played first six games of junior season before suffering a collarbone injury, but returned for final four games of the season; completed 60 percent of his throws and helped Gordo to a 13-2 record and an Alabama 4A state runner-up finish.

name: MJ Morris
scouting_report: A legit 6-foot-1, pushing 6-foot-2. Has tested exceptionally well at different points during his prep career making him a true dual-threat talent. A touch passer. Comfortable throwing on the run, but likes to establish a sturdy base when he can and square up to his target. Some of his best balls can come on quick one and three-step drops. Capable of attacking the deeper parts of the field, but could always find some more velocity and dial in the accuracy. Averaged 6.5 yards per carry as a sophomore. Didn’t have his number called on many designed runs as a junior, but that’s likely due to the fact that he injured his shoulder. Ability to move the chains with his legs and improvise when things break down makes one of the more intriguing quarterback prospects in the class of 2022. Will need to keep developing over the next few years and get more consistent, but has some of the required traits to win games at the Power 5 level. Likely to excel in a spread offense with RPO concepts.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso plays baseball.2020: Transferred into Atlanta (Ga.) Pace Academy. Missed some time due to injury.2019: Started 12 games at Carrollton (Ga.) High where he accounted for 2,565 yards of total offense and 18 touchdowns.

name: Nicco Marchiol
scouting_report: Prototype build and frame. Quick-release left-hander with ability to slide the pocket. Impressive presence and anticipation. Goes through progressions well and shows terrific rhythm to find targets. Steps up when he has to and keeps composure under duress. Quick release with ability to throw on the run. Athletic enough to pick up yards down field. Upside as multi-year starter, All-Conference type performer and NFL Draft Day 3 selection.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundPlayed his freshman and sophomore seasons at Aurora (Colo.) Regis Jesuit before moving to Arizona with his family.2020: Played 8 of 10 games for Chandler (Ariz.) Hamilton as a junior.

name: Noah Fifita
scouting_report: Small frame and short in stature. Limited physically without much growth potential. Dynamic playmaker in the nation's toughest high school league. Shows knack for maintaining eyes down field while scrambling. Displays decent arm strength and good accuracy. Repeatedly throwing mechanics and generates easy torque. Flashes ability to necessary loft on passes into tight spaces. Makes good decisions with the football and puts good touch. Looks in rhythm when stepping up in the pocket to fire down field. Shows wiggle in open space to buy extra yards. Doesn't force throws and tucks the ball to fight for positive plays. Despite diminutive height, stays away from big hits and doesn't go down easily. Could could to improve overall speed and add zip on deep routes. Potential starter at Power Five level later in college career.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundNoah Fifita is a Quarterback from Anaheim, CA.

name: Peter Delaportas
scouting_report: Excellent frame with shoulder width to add 15 pounds. High-level athlete and very good wrestler. Great competitor and leader. Very good at pre-snap reads. Spins ball well and throws catchable ball. Nice touch on deep passes. Trusts arm to get into tight windows. Shows ability to extend plays. Accurate on the move. Needs to work through progressions. Has to continue to improve arm strength. Must get stronger in upper body. Sometimes uses a lot of shoulder movement to add zip to throw. Locks onto receivers. Multi-year starter at Top 20 program. Day 3 NFL draft potential.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundPeter Delaportas is a 6-3, 195-pound Pro-Style Quarterback from Sparta, NJ.

name: Braden Davis
scouting_report: Excellent frame with length. Athletic with escapability. Does not panic under pressure. Good pocket awareness. Good arm strength that will improve with maturity. Shows very good touch on intermediate and deep balls. Throws catchable ball. Gets through progressions. Extends plays. Strong work ethic. Good foot speed and feet work in unison with arm. Has to tighten lengthy delivery. Drops elbow at times. Must continue to get stronger and add weight. Added strength will increase arm strength. Multi-year starter at high-major program with long-term NFL Draft potential.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2021: Named Gatorade Player of the Year in Delaware; Led high school to Delaware 3A state championship2020: Suffered an ankle sprain in the third game of his junior season playing at Lake Minneola (Fla.) High and returned to Delaware and enrolled at Middletown High in the winter.2019: Played at Middletown (Del.) HS as a sophomore

name: Cameron Edge
scouting_report: Good frame and arm angle utlitizes it. Has arm strength. Nice touch of short and intermediate throws. Makes good pre-snap reads. Poised and mature. Has mobility and knows how to slide in pocket. Can extend plays. Showed improved arm strength in the spring from freshman season.  Release should speed up as high school career progresses. Does not always step into throws but still shows arm strength. Will learn to get through progressions more. Played varsity as freshman for high level program. Starter for high level Power 5 program.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundPlayed at Hyattsvile (Md.) DeMatha Catholic prior to junior season.Spring 2021: Played abbreviated spring season as a sophomore for DeMatha.

name: Adryan Lara
scouting_report: Broad-shouldered frame with strong lower half. Strong-armed quarterback with ability to air it out downfield. Shows nice touch on deep throws. Creates a lot of torque with his upper body and can sling ball from different angles. Displays pocket awareness and ability to make pass-rushers miss. Can extend plays while keeping his eyes down field. Delivers the ball on time and leads receivers into space. Stands in the pocket while facing pressure, but at times could make better decisions. Could also continue to refine drop-back footwork while shortening long wind-up motion. Projects as eventual Power Five starter with chance to garner NFL Draft attention.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2019: Helped Desert Edge to a 10-4 record and Arizona 4A state runner-up finish as a sophomore.

name: Arch Manning
scouting_report: Possesses an ideal frame at 6-foot-4 and 215-pounds and growth potential to add additional 10 to 15-pounds to his frame at the next level. Displays above average athleticism and adequate mobility as a runner, increasing his rushing totals every season over the last three years. Appears to have good pocket awareness / presence and the ability to extend plays with his feet. Has enough mobility as a runner to have to respect him in situational down and distance areas. Exhibits clean feet in the pocket while demonstrating good foot quickness and play tempo in his drop. Displays excellent arm talent, showing the ability to make every throw on the field within the pocket. Shows the ability to drive the ball down the field and play with velocity. Excels as an intermediate passer, displaying the ability to consistently deliver the ball accurately and on time while playing with anticipation. Throws an exceptional deep ball, flashing outstanding touch and ball placement allowing him to throw his receivers open. Can play rushed under duress at times, affecting his accuracy in decision making in the short to intermediate part of the field. Will need to continue to improve on making the “right” decision when facing pressure, as he can force himself into making unnecessary passes when his clock is sped up. In addition to possessing all the physical tools desired at the quarterback position, Manning plays the game with exceptional intuition and awareness, showing the ability to command and navigate the game at a very high level. Entering his fourth season as a starter as a senior at Newman, Manning figures to be prepared to make an immediate impact at the next level. Projects as an all-american prospect at the next level with the potential to have his name be one of the first called in the NFL Draft.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso plays basketball.2021: As a junior, led New Orleans Isidore Newman to a 7-3 record and a Louisiana Division III state semifinal appearance.2020: Led New Orleans Isidore Newman to a 9-1 record and Louisiana Division III state semifinal berth as a sophomore.2019: As a freshman, led New Orleans Isidore Newman to a 9-2 record and second-round playoff appearance in Louisiana's Division III bracket.

name: Nico Iamaleava
scouting_report: Former five-star recruit with elite physical tools and one of the highest ceilings in college football. A long, athletic frame paired with top-end arm strength makes Iamaleava a prototypical modern quarterback from a traits standpoint. Showed flashes of brilliance during Tennessee’s 2024 College Football Playoff run, but overall passing production remained modest (77th nationally in passing offense) in a system that leaned heavily on the run game and defense. Plays best when allowed to extend plays and improvise, but struggles to consistently operate within structure. Decision-making and timing remain works in progress, particularly on intermediate throws and against disguised coverage. Can be streaky with accuracy and doesn’t always play on schedule, but has shown the ability to generate explosive plays when things break down. Intangibles — including poise, anticipation, and leadership consistency — are still developing. That said, the upside remains massive. With continued growth in processing speed and pocket discipline, Iamaleava has the natural ability to elevate a contender and play his way into Round 1 of the NFL Draft.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundMember of 247Sports' 2023 Freaks List.Also a top volleyball recruit and has an older brother on volleyball team at Long Beach State. Played his sophomore season at Downey (Calif.) Warren before moving back to Long Beach (Calif.) Poly.2021: As a junior threw for 2,244 yards, 33 TD, and one INT on a 59.6 completion percentage (162 of 272). Added 158 yards and three TD on 4.9 ypc. Led Warren to an 8-1 record and a CIF Southern Section Division 2 playoff appearance.

name: Dante Moore
scouting_report: Varsity starter since his freshman season. Has played well against top competition and in clutch situations in big games. Mature, polished, does not appear to rattle easily. Stands in against the rush and will deliver throws while taking hits. Does a nice job of reading coverages and does well throwing into windows against zone coverages. Anticipates well and gets rid of the ball quickly. Throws with good touch down the field. Can fire it in there when he needs to. Has good arm strength but would not classify the pure velocity as elite. Very consistent with ball placement and mechanics as we have seen very few wayward passes from him through three years of game and camp eval. Can throw receivers open and gives his playmakers a chance to go get the ball. Solid athlete who has added more mobility to his game as the years have gone on, but is not likely to be a big threat as a designed runner, but can do a little bit of that if needed. Physical traits are good, but it is his intangible traits that are elite as well as his accuracy and understanding of the game. Works extremely hard and that total package projects him to be a multi-year college starter and someone who can be an early draft choice. Very ready to play as a true freshman.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundFinished high school career with a 40-11 record across four seasons, winning two state titles and appearing in another state championship game.-----2022: As a senior, led Detroit King to a 10-3 record and a second consecutive Michigan Division III state championship.2021: As a junior, led Detroit King to a 13-1 record and the Michigan Division III state championship.2020: As a sophomore, led Detroit King to a 6-4 record and a Michigan Division III fourth-round playoff appearance.2019: Led Detroit King to an 11-3 record and a Michigan Division II state runner-up finish as a freshman.- Gabe Brooks

name: Jackson Arnold
scouting_report: Adequate height with the requisite frame and solid build. Functional athlete with enough suddenness in tight quarters to evade pass rushers and extend plays. RPO threat. Ball gets out fast with good velocity to multiple levels. Very good touch passer, whether vertically to the perimeter or elsewhere. Displays the requisite deep-ball strength fostered by a loose, live arm. Changes delivery angle as needed and maintains zip and accuracy. Shows ability to layer throws and beat zone windows. Good runner with above average speed relative to position and open-field lateral nuance that yields extra yards. Advanced pocket presence and rush awareness. Uses the inverted pivot to buy time and find receivers. Accurate from the pocket and on the move. Owns the desired demeanor for the position. Calm, poised, and competitive. Strong production vs. elite competition relative to the high school level (Texas 6A). Led team to Texas 6A D-II state title appearance as a junior. Values the ball. Only five interceptions in 445 throws as a junior (one INT every 89 attempts). Big enough but lacks elite frame potential. Deep shots occasionally have too much air. Through 2021 season appears to be the clear No. 1 QB in Texas for the 2023 recruiting cycle. Projects as a high-major starter for multiple years with a long-term NFL Draft ceiling.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso plays baseball.2021: Texas District 5-6A Co-MVP as a junior. Helped lead Guyer to the Class 6A-Division II state championship game2020: Second-string QB behind Texas A&M 2021 four-star signee Eli Stowers.2019: Forced into action as a freshman in the first quarter of the Texas 6A D-II state championship game vs. Austin Westlake (24-3 loss) when Eli Stowers got injured.-- Gabe Brooks

name: Malachi Nelson
scouting_report: Tall, lean, and long-armed with slender natural build but frame to add some mass. Fluid mover in general, including arm motion and lower-body mechanics. Range of motion in movement patterns fosters excellent off-platform passing ability and general playmaking acumen. Alters arm angles and delivers with accuracy. Vertical arm strength, intermediate velocity, and touch to all fields speak to elite arm talent. Perhaps the most natural thrower in an excellent 2023 QB group. Flashes the functional athleticism to extend plays outside the pocket, where finding a receiver is almost always the priority over tucking and running. Still, athletic enough to hurt defenses with legs on occasion. Sees the field well and processes naturally. Production has risen each of first three years in high school. INT rate is on the higher end of the spectrum for Top247 quarterbacks -- one every 30.6 throws as a junior, one every 29.9 in freshman through junior seasons. Adding mass and strength will help withstand physical demands of high-major ball. Very likely one of the nation's top quarterbacks -- and overall prospects regardless of position -- in the 2023 class. Projects as an impact starter at the high-major level with long-term early-round NFL Draft potential.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2022: 6-foot-3, 180 poundsFall 2021: Led Los Alamitos to a 9-2 record as a junior, averaging 244.5 passing yards per game with a 39-9 TD-INT ratio.Spring 2021: Posted a 23-4 TD-INT ratio as a sophomore during an abbreviated six-game season.2019: Accounted for almost 1,000 total yards of offense on varsity as a freshman.

name: Jaden Rashada
scouting_report: Owns a frame and build that can add ample weight and strength. Has the mold to add at least 20 pounds of muscle easily. As he matures and gets stronger it will benefit his durability, long-term ability, and development. Stands tall in the pocket with the mobility to avoid the rush and buy time to make plays under pressure. Poised and does a nice job making plays off platform. Mechanically displays a smooth, effortless throwing motion. Gets the ball out quickly, generating power from his lower body to have the ball snap out of his hand. Does a good job changing speeds and adding touch  on throws when need be, to either fit the pass into tight windows or to complete throws over defenders. Good ball placement on passes where only the receiver is able to make the play. Plays with impressive anticipation on his throws where you can see that he is throwing his receivers open and out of harm's way. Continued development should foster growth in his ability to go through his progressions and understand pre-snap what coverage the defense is in so that he can get the ball out quicker than he already does.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundPlayed freshman season (2019) at Brentwood (Calif.) Liberty before moving to IMG Academy in Bradenton, Fla. Moved to Pittsburg (Calif.) for an abbreviated five-game sophomore season in Spring 2021. Also has played baseball during high school career.

name: Aidan Chiles
scouting_report: Chiles is a true dual-threat quarterback who has made big strides in his game over the last year. After breaking his wrist halfway through his junior season, Chiles put up big numbers as a senior and can stress a defense with his arm and his legs. He has an easy delivery and the ball jumps out of his hand. He’s elusive in the pocket, runs well and is very capable of picking up chunks of yards on zone reads or escaping pressure and taking off. He has worked hard on his mechanics and his release is tighter and he’s able to get the ball off from different arm angles. The game is slowing down for him and you can see he’s processing faster and able to find secondary receivers. He’s a natural leader and will be a future captain at the next level and as his game will only progress as he takes more game reps.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso plays basketball.Fall 2021: Played six games as a junior. Threw for 1,301 yards with a 10-2 TD-INT ratio while completing 73.8 percent of his attempts. Also added 31-344-4 rushing on 11.1 yards per carry.Spring 2021: Played a pandemic-abbreviated five-game sophomore spring season. Completed 9 of 11 attempts for 114 yards, two TDs, and 0 INTs. Added 4-16-0 rushing.- Gabe Brooks

name: Avery Johnson
scouting_report: Verified at 6-foot-2 and 173 pounds you see that he has ample room to add weight and get physically stronger which will only enhance his overall talent and skill level. I look for him to add the majority of his growth in his upper body. His lower half is well defined and that of a sprinter. With good length to his frame Johnson is a top-flight athlete at the QB position. He has good speed and change of direction which he uses to his advantage when he is on the move and out of the pocket. He is light on his feet and able to maneuver around the pocket with ease as he possesses very good pocket presence and awareness. He is a threat to pull the ball down and run at any moment which is a constant point of stress for a defense. As a passer he has improved his mechanics which will allow for him to reach his full potential. With an easy smooth delivery the ball comes out with zip and on a line especially on the short and intermediate routes. In regards to the deep ball he has enough arm to stretch the field and get it vertical to receivers down field. The accuracy part of his game has improved as well as the ability to anticipate where the receivers will be in regards to coverages that he may face and allowing him to throw guys open. Long-term potential is definitely there as he best football is ahead of him. The tools and the work ethic make him a player that once the talent and production match he will have a successful career. I see him as a multiple year power-5 starter with an NFL future ahead of him when it’s all said and done.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundMember of 247Sports' 2023 Freaks List.Two-sport athlete who shows some bounce on the basketball floor.Averaged 11.6 points, 2.7 rebounds, 3.7 assists, 2.2 steals, and 1.0 blocks per game as a junior for a Maize team that went 18-6 and reached the state semifinals in Kansas 5A.Leading scorer as a sophomore for a Maize squad that went 23-2 and won the Kansas 5A state championship. Averaged 13.2 points, 4.4 rebounds, 3.2 assists, and 1.8 steals on 49 percent shooting and 36 percent from three-point range.2021: As a junior, led Maize to an 11-3 record and the Kansas 5A state championship game, where Maize fell 28-14 to Mill Valley.2020: Led Maize to a 9-2 record as a sophomore starting QB.- Gabe Brooks

name: Christopher Vizzina
scouting_report: Possesses adequate size and frame for the position, verified at 6-foot-3 and 200-pounds plus. Has the frame to add additional weight to his body at the next level. Displays good athleticism in the pocket and above average mobility as a runner. Appears to have a loose compact throwing motion. Shows the ability to get the ball out from a variety of angles. Exhibits good foot quickness and the ability to reset his feet in the pocket. Has the tendency to play rushed at times in the pocket but flashes good play tempo and ability to extend plays with his feet. Possesses good arm strength and velocity. Ball pops off his hand. Excels throwing the deep ball with touch and accuracy. Needs to continue to add some polish in the short to intermediate part of his game, but flashes the accuracy to be a consistent passer at the next level. Above average passer that has the ability to develop into a good passer at the next level. Provides a threat with his legs, not only as a runner, but with his ability to extend plays outside the pocket and throw off platform. Projects to a multi-year Power Five starter at the next level. Will need two years to develop before seeing credible playing time.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic Background2022: Completed 64.1% of passing attempts at Briarwood High School during his senior campaign.2021: Led Briarwood High School (6A) to a 10-2 season.

name: Lincoln Kienholz
scouting_report: Athletic prospect who can make plays with his feet. Uses that athleticism to navigate the pocket and elude the rush. Can run, but will look to throw when he breaks contain. Throws with excellent touch. Drops passes into a bucket, gives his receivers opportunities to make plays on the ball and leads them away from coverage on vertical routes or corner routes. Productive in high school and saw some good programs but did not face many teams with D1 prospects in the secondary so he will have to adjust to the speed and windows shrinking quicker in college. Has good but not arm strength and velocity. Is going to fill in more in college and add some of that though. Far from a finished product but has a lot of good developmental tools, is a gamer, and mobility makes him fit well with today's offenses. Should develop into a Power Five starter and possible pro.
athletic_background: H.S. Athletic BackgroundAlso plays basketball and baseball.Finished junior baseball season batting .472 with six HR and went 3-0 with a 1.24 ERA.South Dakota 2A all-state basketball selection as a junior after averaging 19.9 points, 7.3 rebounds, and 2.8 assists per game.-----2022: MaxPreps South Dakota High School Player of the Year as a senior. Threw for 3,422 yards with 46-6 TD-INT ratio on 66.5 completion percentage. Ran for 1,436 yards and 24 TDs. Added 12 tackles and four INTs on defense.2021: In 12 games as a junior, threw for 3,359 yards, 37 TDs, and six INTs while completing 55.0 percent of attempts. Added 167-1,165-12 rushing on 7.0 ypc.
